title: 2 Bedroom Houses for Rent on Cleveland's East Side
metaTitle: 2 Bedroom Houses for Rent Cleveland East Side
metaDescription: Browse 2 bedroom houses for rent on Cleveland's east side — ZIPs 44105-44120, transit, rent ranges, and Section 8-friendly homes. Book a showing today.
primaryKeyword: 2 bedroom houses for rent cleveland east side
secondaryKeywords: cleveland east side houses for rent
answerBox: Two-bedroom houses on Cleveland's east side — Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Hough, Fairfax, and Buckeye-Shaker — typically rent from about $750 to $1,100 a month. The east side is where we work with the largest share of our rental homes, and every one accepts Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers.

sections.h2: 2-bedroom houses for rent on Cleveland's east side
body: Cleveland's east side runs from Downtown out through University Circle, Hough, Fairfax, Glenville, Collinwood, and southeast into Buckeye-Shaker, Mount Pleasant, and Slavic Village. It is the part of the city where we work with the largest share of our rental homes, concentrated in ZIP codes **44105 (Slavic Village/Union-Miles), 44110 (Collinwood), 44108 (Glenville), 44104 (Central/Fairfax), 44103 (Hough), and 44120 (Buckeye-Shaker)**.

Two-bedroom homes are the single most common size in our east side selection. If you're searching for a 2-bedroom house on the east side, [book a showing](/p/book-showing) and tell us your target neighborhood, budget, and move-in date — we'll match you against current availability rather than a fixed list that goes stale.

sections.h2: How much does a 2-bedroom house rent for on the east side?
body: Across our currently available east side inventory, 2-bedroom homes typically run **$750 to $1,100 a month**, averaging just under $1,000. That tracks below the citywide 2-bedroom median reported by Zumper for July 2026 and well below the larger professionally-managed apartment complexes that RentCafe tracks separately — both of those sources cover Cleveland overall, not our specific east-side selection, so treat the table below as two different lenses on the same market.

rows: Median asking rent, all Cleveland listings
Average of professionally-managed apartment complexes, 1,027 sq ft

body: The east side is well served by Greater Cleveland RTA. The **Red Line** heavy-rail runs from Cleveland Hopkins Airport through Downtown's Tower City hub out to Windermere in East Cleveland, and the **Blue and Green light-rail lines** run from Tower City out to Shaker Heights along Shaker Boulevard. The **HealthLine** bus-rapid-transit route runs the length of Euclid Avenue connecting Public Square, University Circle, and East Cleveland in a dedicated median busway.

University Circle — home to Case Western Reserve University, the Cleveland Museum of Art, Severance Music Center, and the Cleveland Clinic's main campus — sits roughly in the middle of the east side, making it a common reference point for commute distance whether you're looking near Glenville, Hough, or Fairfax.

items: **Rail:** Red Line (airport–Windermere) and Blue/Green Lines to Shaker Heights, all meeting at Tower City
**Bus rapid transit:** HealthLine on Euclid Avenue, Public Square to University Circle to East Cleveland
**Landmarks:** University Circle institutions, Shaker Square, Morgana Run Trail in Slavic Village

body: Every home we work with — on the east side and elsewhere — accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is kept HUD-inspection-ready. Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) administers the voucher program locally and accepts preliminary [Housing Choice Voucher applications](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) online year-round, though placement runs on a random mini-lottery system rather than strict first-come, first-served, so wait times vary.

One important honesty note: **the City of Cleveland itself does not have a source-of-income ordinance** requiring landlords to accept vouchers — that protection currently exists only in a handful of Cuyahoga County suburbs (Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale), not in Cleveland proper. We accept vouchers on every unit by choice, but it's worth knowing that isn't a citywide legal requirement. See our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full picture.

sections.h2: What a 2-bedroom east side rental typically looks like
body: Housing stock on the east side leans early-1900s to mid-century, with a mix of **single-family houses, duplexes, and small multi-family buildings** — full basements and small yards are common, especially in Slavic Village and Mount Pleasant. Our current selection reflects that same mix rather than an all-detached-house lineup, so when you [browse Cleveland houses for rent](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) expect both standalone houses and duplex units listed as "houses."

faq.q: What ZIP codes on Cleveland's east side have the most 2-bedroom houses for rent?
faq.a: Our largest east side concentrations are in ZIP codes 44105 (Slavic Village), 44110 (Collinwood), 44108 (Glenville), 44104 (Central/Fairfax), 44103 (Hough), and 44120 (Buckeye-Shaker). Availability shifts week to week, so book a showing to see current 2-bedroom options in any of these areas.

faq.q: Do east side 2-bedroom rentals accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Yes. Every home we work with, including our 2-bedroom east side inventory, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Note that Cleveland itself has no source-of-income ordinance forcing acceptance citywide, so voucher-friendliness varies by landlord — ours is a standing policy.

faq.q: How much is rent for a 2-bedroom house on Cleveland's east side?
faq.a: Our current east side 2-bedroom availability runs roughly $750 to $1,100 a month, averaging around $961. Zumper's July 2026 citywide median for 2-bedrooms is $1,100, while RentCafe's average for larger managed apartment complexes is considerably higher at $1,818.

faq.q: What transit serves east side rentals in Cleveland?
faq.a: The RTA Red Line runs from the airport through Downtown to East Cleveland, the Blue and Green Lines connect Downtown to Shaker Heights, and the HealthLine bus-rapid-transit route runs along Euclid Avenue through University Circle. All rail lines meet at the Tower City hub Downtown.

faq.q: Are east side houses mostly single-family or multi-family?
faq.a: It's a mix. East side housing stock includes standalone single-family houses alongside duplexes and small multi-family buildings, often with basements and yards. Our rental listings reflect that same mix rather than exclusively detached houses.
